title: Topology
statement:
A \emph{topology} on a set $X$ is a collection $\tau$ of subsets of $X$
satisfying:
- [label=(\roman*)]
- $\varnothing \in \tau$ and $X \in \tau$;
- if $\{U_\alpha\}_{\alpha \in I}$ is any family of sets in $\tau$ (indexed
 by any set $I$), then $\bigcup_{\alpha \in I} U_\alpha \in \tau$;
- if $U_1, \dots, U_n \in \tau$ (finitely many), then $U_1 \cap \cdots
 \cap U_n \in \tau$.
The pair $(X, \tau)$ is a \emph{topological space}. Members of $\tau$ are
called \emph{open sets}.
